flashcards.back: Unguided media (wireless transmission media) transmits data without physical cables, using electromagnetic waves through air, vacuum, or water.

flashcards.front: Define frequency range of radio waves
flashcards.back: Radio waves range from 3 kHz to 300 GHz. They can penetrate walls and travel long distances.
Line-of-sight propagation requires direct path between transmitter and receiver without obstacles; used by microwaves and infrared.
Sky wave propagation uses ionosphere reflection to transmit signals over long distances; used by AM radio and shortwave broadcasts.
Ground wave follows Earth's curvature, used for long-wave radio transmissions; signal travels along the ground surface.

flashcards.front: What is the frequency range of microwaves?
flashcards.back: Microwaves range from 300 MHz to 300 GHz, requiring line-of-sight communication.
Infrared uses IR rays (300 GHz to 400 THz), requires line-of-sight, cannot penetrate walls; used in TV remotes.
Satellite communication uses microwave signals relayed through artificial satellites in orbit; used for TV, telephony, internet.

flashcards.front: What is the advantage of satellite communication?
flashcards.back: Satellites provide wide coverage, can reach remote areas, and offer broadcast capabilities.
GPS (Global Positioning System) uses satellite signals to provide location and time information anywhere on Earth.
Bluetooth is a short-range wireless technology (2.4 GHz) for device-to-device communication, using FHSS.
Wi-Fi (IEEE 802.11) operates in 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands; standards include 802.11b/g/n/ac/ax.

flashcards.front: What is the advantage of radio waves over microwaves?
flashcards.back: Radio waves can penetrate walls, travel longer distances, and do not require line-of-sight.

flashcards.front: What is the disadvantage of infrared?
flashcards.back: Infrared cannot penetrate walls or solid objects and requires line-of-sight between transmitter and receiver.
FHSS rapidly switches carrier frequency among many channels, providing security and resistance to interference.
